title: Coastal-scrub close-look and atlas phase
summary: Coastal-scrub has zero close-look entries while forest has five. This wave adds 2-3 coastal-scrub close-look candidates that teach visual detail about transition-zone species, plus atlas-facing journal richness for the comparison surface.

execution_order.why_now: Coastal-scrub is the only biome with zero close-look support.
Close-look is a proven visual-teaching surface that coastal-scrub is entirely missing.
Close-look entries need visual and science review.

guardrails: Close-look candidates must be visually interesting species with detail worth zooming into.
Prefer species that teach transition-zone ecology when examined closely.
Do not add more than 3 candidates — close-look should stay occasional.
Callout text must be concise and kid-readable.
